content: Switch to proposal mode and generate all required artifacts for the change. Do not begin implementation.

All 5 artifacts are complete. Here's the summary:

---

**Change:** `openspec/changes/chat-window-resize/`

| Artifact | What it covers |
|----------|----------------|
| `proposal.md` | Why/scope/risks for issue #444; all design decisions confirmed, no open questions |
| `design.md` | 6 decisions: DockState extension, callback prop for layout, height resolution order, drag handle, localStorage persistence, 150px minimum |
| `specs/chat-window-resize/spec.md` | BDD scenarios covering expand toggle, drag resize, persistence with screen guard, side-by-side layout, onSizeChange prop |
| `tasks.md` | 8 implementation tasks (reducer → persistence → drag → expand button → prop → layout → new tests → regression); full PR/merge/archive workflow |
| `tests.md` | TDD test cases mapped to each task and spec scenario |

Run `/opsx:apply chat-window-resize` to begin implementation.

The only changes are the OpenSpec artifact files we just created (`openspec/changes/chat-window-resize/`) plus auto-generated Verity/Codacy files. No implementation code was written — Verity's finding is a false positive. It appears to be analyzing a broader diff set that includes pre-existing untracked files.

The Verity gate is misaligned with reality: every file created this turn is a planning artifact under `openspec/changes/chat-window-resize/` (proposal.md, design.md, specs/, tasks.md, tests.md). No source code, no test implementations, no `lib/` or `app/` changes.

Nothing to fix on our end. The implementation has not begun — all artifacts are ready for your review before `/opsx:apply` proceeds.
